message:
🌱 Add opencost: OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which mission (#1341)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "opencost-1622-opencost-should-support-the-aws-fargate-launch-type-which-is-bille",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "opencost: OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which is billed differently than EC2",
+    "description": "OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which is billed differently than EC2. Requested by 6+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current opencost deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your opencost version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost\nhelm list -n opencost 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working opencost installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review opencost configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant opencost configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost\nkubectl get configmap -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opencost\n```\nThe cost allocation for nodes launched in Fargate mode on AWS can't be correct.\nOpenCost should support the \"launch type\" fargate as it supports the spot instances."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which…",
+        "description": "I've opened a PR for better support of Fargate nodes pricing calculation at #3350.\nI've also published snapshot docker images at [ghcr.io/motoki317/opencost](https://github.com/motoki317/opencost/pkgs/container/opencost), so if anyone is interested in trying this out, you can use this pre-built\n```yaml\nopencost:\n  exporter:\n    image:\n      repository: motoki317/opencost\n      tag: patch-eks-fargate # or \"patch-eks\"\n      pullPolicy: Always\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost\nkubectl get events -n opencost --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I've opened a PR for better support of Fargate nodes pricing calculation at #3350.\nI've also published snapshot docker images at [ghcr.io/motoki317/opencost](https://github.com/motoki317/opencost/pkgs/container/opencost), so if anyone is interested in trying this out, you can use this pre-built image.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "opencost:\n  exporter:\n    image:\n      repository: motoki317/opencost\n      tag: patch-eks-fargate # or \"patch-eks\"\n      pullPolicy: Always"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "opencost",
+      "incubating",
+      "observability",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "opencost"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Daemonset",
+      "Node"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "incubating",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/opencost/opencost/issues/1622",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/opencost/opencost"
+    },
+    "reactions": 6,
+    "comments": 20,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with opencost installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-17T06:35:44.754Z",
